activities.name: Check in — Kyoto Hotel
activities.notes: Drop bags, freshen up.

activities.name: Walk to Nishiki Market
activities.notes: 18 min · 1.2 km · Head southwest from the hotel toward the covered arcade

activities.name: Nishiki Market
activities.notes: Kyoto's 'Kitchen' — narrow covered market with tofu, pickles, street snacks, and local ingredients. 5 blocks long.

activities.name: Weekenders Coffee
activities.notes: One of Kyoto's best specialty coffee spots. Small, cozy, excellent pour-overs.

activities.name: Walk to Pontocho Alley
activities.notes: 10 min · 0.7 km · Head east toward the river

activities.name: Pontocho Alley Stroll
activities.notes: Narrow lantern-lit alley along the Kamo River. One of Kyoto's most atmospheric streets.

activities.name: 🍴 Option A: Honke Daiichi-Asahi
activities.notes: Legendary Kyoto ramen — shoyu broth, thin noodles, char siu. Near Kyoto Station (20 min south from hotel — requires detour).
activities.place: Honke Daiichi Asahi, Kyoto Station, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: 🍴 Option B: Ramen Kairikiya Kawaramachi Sanjo
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Kyoto-style shoyu ramen — light transparent chicken broth with Kujo green onions. On your street, open until 3 AM. ~¥800.
activities.place: Ramen Kairikiya, Kawaramachi Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: 🍴 Option C: Mendokoro Janomeya
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Rich chicken paitan ramen topped with karaage fried chicken. Michelin Guide listed. 1-min walk from Kawaramachi Station.

activities.name: 🍴 Option D: Wajoryomen Sugari
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Refined Japanese-style ramen with delicate broth. Slightly upscale feel, highly rated. Near hotel.

activities.name: 🍴 Chao Chao Gyoza
activities.notes: Late-night gyoza spot near Gion. Crispy pan-fried dumplings, cold beer.
activities.place: Chao Chao Gyoza, Gion, Kyoto, Japan

days.note: ⚡ SWAPPED with May 17: Moved to Saturday, all spots open

activities.name: Keihan Train: Sanjo → Fushimi Inari
activities.notes: 20 min · Keihan Main Line from Sanjo Station to Fushimi Inari Station (2 stops south)

activities.name: ⛩ Fushimi Inari Taisha
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Moved to 07:00 (was 09:00). Open 24/7, free entry. Arrive early to hike the thousands of vermilion torii gates before tour buses arrive at 9. The full loop is 2-3 hours but you can turn back at the viewpoint (~1h).

activities.name: Walk to Tofuku-ji
activities.notes: 15 min · 1.1 km · Head north through the residential lanes

activities.name: Tofuku-ji Temple
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: 09:30 start (was 11:00). 15-min walk from Fushimi Inari. Zen gardens, Tsutenkyo Bridge, Hojo garden — allow 1–1.5 hours. ¥1,000 combo ticket for everything. May = green maples, peaceful without autumn crowds.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch Option A: Kendonya
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Hearty handmade udon & soba. Popular with locals, short walk from Keihan Fushimi Inari Station.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch Option B: Dragon Burger Tofukuji
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Kyoto-inspired gourmet burgers by London competition winner. 1-min walk from Tofukuji Station. Closed Wednesdays.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch Option C: Vermillion Café
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Cozy café near Fushimi Inari JR station. Coffee, pastries, light meals. Nice outdoor back deck for warm weather.

activities.name: Bus to Kiyomizu-dera
activities.notes: 15 min · City Bus 202 toward Higashiyama, alight at Kiyomizumichi stop

activities.name: Kiyomizu-dera
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Moved earlier (was 14:00). Views from the massive wooden stage. Walk downhill through Sannenzaka and Ninenzaka streets after.

activities.name: Sannenzaka & Ninenzaka Streets
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Beautifully preserved stone-paved lanes downhill from Kiyomizu. Traditional shops, tea houses, Yasaka Pagoda views. Flows naturally into Gion.

activities.name: Walk to Kennin-ji
activities.notes: 10 min · 0.7 km · Follow the lane northwest from Sannenzaka into Gion

activities.name: Kennin-ji Temple
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Oldest Zen temple in Kyoto (founded 1202), right inside Gion. Stunning twin dragons ceiling painting, beautiful raked gravel gardens, and screen paintings. ¥800 at gate, no reservation needed.

activities.name: Gion District
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Starts after Kennin-ji (was 16:00). Stroll down Hanamikoji Street — best chance to spot a geisha or maiko heading to evening appointments around dusk.

activities.name: Walk to Pontocho
activities.notes: 15 min · 0.9 km · Head west from Hanamikoji through Gion toward the river

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner Option A: Pontocho Alley Izakaya
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Pick any riverside izakaya along the lantern-lit alley. Yakitori, tempura, Kyoto cuisine with Kamo River views.

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner Option B: Gion Duck Noodles
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Popular duck ramen spot right in Gion. Rich duck broth, always has a queue but worth the wait.

days.note: ⚡ SWAPPED with May 16: Otagi Nenbutsuji is closed Saturdays, moved Arashiyama to Sunday

activities.name: Travel to Arashiyama
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Earlier start to catch Bamboo Grove empty at 7 AM. Walk to Sanjo Keihan Station → Subway Tozai Line West to Uzumasa Tenjingawa → Randen Tram to Arashiyama. (~35 mins)

activities.name: 🎍 Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Moved from old May 18. 7 AM gives a magical, empty window. Right next door: Tenryu-ji Temple stunning gardens.

activities.name: Katsura River & Togetsukyo Bridge
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Moved from old May 18. Cross the scenic Togetsukyo Bridge. Grab a coffee at % ARABICA Kyoto Arashiyama and enjoy the waterfront views.

activities.name: Arashiyama Monkey Park Iwatayama
activities.notes: Hike up (20 mins), see monkeys, view the city. Walk 5 mins from station to entrance.
activities.place: Iwatayama Monkey Park, Arashiyama, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: 🍴 Arashiyama Yoshimura
activities.notes: Soba with river view, or street food near the bridge.

activities.name: Taxi to Otagi Nenbutsu-ji
activities.notes: Taxi approx ¥1,500 from Arashiyama main street. Walking takes 45 mins uphill — taxi is better.

activities.name: Otagi Nenbutsuji Temple
activities.notes: Hidden gem with 1,200 statues. Quiet and atmospheric.
activities.place: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji, Sagadaikakujimonzen Toriimoto, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, Japan

activities.name: Saga-Toriimoto
activities.notes: Walk downhill from Otagi back through the Saga-Toriimoto preserved street. Beautiful and far less crowded than the Bamboo Grove.

activities.name: JR to Kyoto Station + quick bite
activities.notes: JR Saga-Arashiyama → Kyoto Station (~17 min). Grab something to eat before teamLab — Kyoto Station Ramen Koji (10th floor) or convenience store bento on the platform. teamLab is a 10-min walk from Hachijo East Gate.

activities.name: Walk to teamLab Biovortex
activities.notes: 15 min · 1.3 km · Exit Kyoto Station Hachijo East Gate, walk south

activities.name: teamLab Biovortex Kyoto
activities.notes: Japan's largest teamLab — opened Oct 2025. 10,000 sqm, 50+ artworks. Much bigger and newer than anything in Tokyo. 10-min walk from Kyoto Station Hachijo East Gate. Book in advance, sells out. Allow 3 hours.

activities.name: Subway back to Sanjo
activities.notes: 15 min · Subway Karasuma Line from Kyoto Station north to Marutamachi, then short walk east to hotel area

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner
activities.notes: Head back toward the hotel — Pontocho, Nishiki area, or Kawaramachi for dinner. Plenty of options within 5-min walk of the hotel.

days.note: ⚡ CHANGED: Nara & Uji day trip (moved from May 19 for more time)

activities.name: Train: Kyoto → Nara
activities.notes: 45 min · JR Nara Line from Kyoto Station to JR Nara Station (~¥740)

activities.name: Nara Day Trip
activities.notes: ⚡ MOVED from May 19. JR Nara Line from Kyoto (~45 min). Todai-ji (giant Buddha), deer in Nara Park, Kasuga Taisha Shrine. Full morning = no rushing.

activities.name: Train: Nara → Uji
activities.notes: 35 min · JR Nara Line from JR Nara Station to Uji Station (~¥470)

activities.name: Uji Day Trip
activities.notes: ⚡ MOVED from May 19. JR Nara Line from Nara to Uji (~30 min). Byodoin Temple (10-yen coin), Uji Bridge, matcha street, matcha ice cream. More relaxed than the original 1.5h slot.

activities.name: Return to Kyoto
activities.notes: JR from Uji back to Kyoto (~20 min). Last evening in Kyoto — relax and enjoy.

activities.name: Subway: Kyoto Station → Sanjo area
activities.notes: 10 min · Subway Karasuma Line north to Marutamachi, then walk east to the river

activities.name: Kamo River sunset stroll
activities.notes: Locals sit along the Kamo River banks at dusk — one of Kyoto's most beloved free experiences. Grab a convenience store drink, find a spot on the steps, and watch the city wind down. Completely free, completely local.

activities.name: 🍴 Last dinner in Kyoto
activities.notes: Last night — make it count. Revisit a favorite from earlier in the trip, or try somewhere new in Pontocho or Gion.

days.note: ⚡ CHANGED: Kinkakuji/Ryoanji morning → Departure to Osaka

activities.name: Bus: Hotel → Kinkaku-ji
activities.notes: 35 min · City Bus 12 from Kawaramachi-Sanjo stop toward Kinugasa, alight at Kinkakujicho

activities.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
activities.notes: ⚡ MOVED from May 18. Iconic gold-leaf Zen temple on a reflective pond. ~30-45 min visit. Opens 9:00.

activities.name: Walk to Ryoan-ji
activities.notes: 15 min · 1.1 km · Head southwest along the wooded path

activities.name: Ryoan-ji Temple
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Famous Zen rock garden — 15 rocks on raked white gravel, designed so you can never see all 15 at once. Quieter and more meditative than Kinkakuji. 15 min walk or short bus from Kinkakuji.

activities.name: Bus: Ryoan-ji → Kyoto Station
activities.notes: 30 min · City Bus 50 or 205 from Ryoanji-mae stop south to Kyoto Station

activities.name: Travel to Osaka
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Now departing from Kyoto (was via Uji). JR Special Rapid from Kyoto Station to Osaka (~30 min). Check in to Hotel Forza Osaka Namba.
